lesson: When the refrigerant line is freezing cold but the product stays warm, the evaporator cylinder is insulated. Check for a buildup of sugar scale on the cylinder and scrub it off.
symptoms: Slushie is taking hours to freeze down.
Compressor is running hot and long.
Bowls look full and augers are turning.

tests.temp-probe: Refrigerant line is 15°F (-9°C), but slush mix is stuck at 39°F (4°C).
tests.inspect-beater: Auger removed. The outer surface of the stainless steel evaporator cylinder is coated in a thick, crusty layer of white sugar scale, insulating the mix from the cold.
tests.continuity-test: Evaporator thermistor resistance matches actual temp. Compressor contactor has continuity.

flavour: Scrubbed the sugar crust. The cold can finally get through.
authenticityNote: If the operators do not scrub the cylinder surface during sanitisation, a microscopic layer of sugar and milk solids (in dairy slushies) or hard water minerals will build up. This scale acts as a thermal insulator, preventing heat exchange between the refrigerant and the slush.
